exploration_log.aha_moment: The fix needed two things: (1) register the type comparator BEFORE rebuilding the ExtendedByTypesComparator so the updated map is passed in, and (2) check if the existing comparator is an ExtendedByTypesComparator to preserve the underlying base comparator.
exploration_log.hindsight: I would read ExtendedByTypesComparator first to understand its two constructors before modifying usingComparatorForType.

alternatives: Only handle null case and ignore non-null case
key_decisions.decision: Only rebuild when the existing comparator is an ExtendedByTypesComparator, skip otherwise
key_decisions.reasoning: The task says to fix specifically when ExtendedByTypesComparator is already in use; non-ExtendedByTypesComparator cases represent a user-set custom comparator that should not be silently replaced

solve_notes: The bug was in `usingComparatorForType`: it only installed an `ExtendedByTypesComparator` when `iterables.getComparator() == null`, so if the user had previously called `usingElementComparator`, the new type comparator was registered in `comparatorsByType` but not applied to the iteration strategy. The fix adds an `else if` branch that detects when an `ExtendedByTypesComparator` is already in use and rebuilds it with the updated type comparators map, preserving the original base comparator.
task_text:
Fix AbstractIterableAssert.usingComparatorForType not applying when element comparator is already set

In `assertj-core/src/main/java/org/assertj/core/api/AbstractIterableAssert.java`,
the `usingComparatorForType(Comparator, Class)` method only installs a
wrapping `ExtendedByTypesComparator` when `iterables.getComparator() == null`.
If the user previously called `usingElementComparator(existingCmp)` (making
`iterables.getComparator()` non-null), a subsequent
`usingComparatorForType(newCmp, MyType.class)` call updates `comparatorsByType`
but does NOT update the `iterables` strategy, so `newCmp` is silently
ignored during element comparison. Fix `usingComparatorForType` to also
update the element comparison strategy when an `ExtendedByTypesComparator`
is already in use, by rebuilding it with the updated `comparatorsByType` map.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: directly exercises the usingComparatorForType method, first call installs ExtendedByTypesComparator (null branch)
calls usingComparatorForType twice which triggers the non-null branch on the second call
